title: AFT Settlement
body_text:
AFT Settlement
1	Introduction to AFT Settlement
1.1	Overview of AFT Settlement
There are two types of AFT Settlement: Outgoing AFT transactions and Incoming AFT transactions. Outgoing AFT transactions created by a financial institution or business member originator are posted to both the financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account and the recipient芒聙聶s account on the due date. Incoming AFT transactions created by another financial institution are posted to both the financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account and the recipient芒聙聶s account on the due date. Transactions received by Central 1 after the due date are posted on the date they are received.
Related Information
AFT Rejects, Recalls, Error Corrections, and Other Exception Items Part
AFT Reports Part
Current Account Services (CAS) Online Part
File Exchange and Report Distribution Part
Introduction to AFT Part
Online Return System (ORS) Part
AFT Returns and AFT Notice of Change Transactions Part
CPA Rule F1, Rules Applicable to Automated Funds Transfer (AFT) Transactions
1.2	Transaction Types Posted
The following list describes the types of incoming and outgoing AFT transactions posted to the accounts of members, originators, and/or financial institutions.
Original Transaction
Created by a financial institution or business member originator 芒聙聯 A preauthorized payment or direct deposit transaction that is created by a financial institution or business member originator that either debits or credits an account at another financial institution. If the transaction is a direct deposit or credit payment, it is referred to as a C record. If the transaction is a pre-authorized debit, it is referred to as a D record. An offsetting entry is posted to the originator芒聙聶s account for the file total. See Offsetting Entry later in this section.
Received by a financial institution member 芒聙聯 A pre-authorized payment or direct deposit transaction created by an originator at another financial institution that credits or debits a financial institution member芒聙聶s account. If the transaction is a direct deposit or credit payment, it is referred to as a C record. If the transaction is a pre-authorized debit, it is referred to as a D record.
Error Correction Transaction
Created by a financial institution or business member originator 芒聙聯 A transaction that reverses the original AFT transaction created by the financial institution or business member originator. If an account at another financial institution is erroneously credited or debited, the error correction transaction reverses the original transaction. A debit reversal that reverses an original credit is an E record. A credit reversal that reverses an original debit is referred to as an F record. For example, a direct deposit sent to an account at another financial institution in error, gets reversed with a debit (an E record).
Received by financial institution member 芒聙聯 A transaction that reverses the original AFT credit or debit to the financial institution member芒聙聶s account. A debit reversal that reverses an original credit is an E record. A credit reversal that reverses an original debit is referred to as an F record. For example, a direct deposit sent to a financial institution member芒聙聶s account in error, gets reversed with a debit (an E record).
Note:	A member can refuse an error correction transaction within 90 days of the posting date. Return the error correction using the transaction type 915 or 922 in accordance with CPA Standard 005 and Standard 007. Transactions refused after this time must be dealt with outside the Clearing.
Returned Transaction
Created by financial institution 芒聙聯 An AFT return is created by the financial institution for an incoming debit or credit transaction that can芒聙聶t be posted. For example, if a pre-authorized payment can芒聙聶t be debited from a financial institution member芒聙聶s account due to insufficient funds, the transaction is returned NSF to the originating financial institution. Credits (C or F records) are returned as credits and referred to as I records. Debits (D or E records) are returned as debits and referred to as J records.
Received by financial institution 芒聙聯 An AFT return received by the financial institution is for a transaction that was returned by the financial institution to which it was originally sent. This is a transaction that the financial institution or business member originated, but could not be posted to the recipient芒聙聶s account. Credits (C or F records) are returned as credits and referred to as I records. Debits (D or E records) are returned as debits and referred to as J records.
Offsetting Entry
Created for AFT file of outgoing transactions originated by financial institution or business member 芒聙聯 A transaction included in the financial institution or business member芒聙聶s outgoing AFT file that offsets the total amount of the file. This transaction is automatically posted to the originator芒聙聶s account for Canadian dollar files. If the financial institution does not have a banking system to facilitate this transaction for US dollar AFT files, the offsetting entry is manually posted.
Created for file of AFT returns originated by financial institution 芒聙聯 A transaction that offsets an AFT item returned through the Online Return System (ORS), or the batch return service if the software does not create the offsetting entry. For example, if a financial institution sends a PAD to a financial institution member芒聙聶s account in which there are insufficient funds, the financial institution returns the transaction to the originating financial institution. The offsetting return entry posts funds back to the member芒聙聶s account and will appear on the Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing (CCD/UCD) report as an I record.
